title: Accessing Shared Network Drives and File Shares
text:
Q: How do I access shared network drives and file shares?
A: To access shared network drives and file shares, follow these steps based on your operating system:

Windows:
1. Open 'File Explorer' by clicking on the folder icon in the taskbar or pressing 'Windows + E'.
2. In the left sidebar, click on 'This PC'.
3. At the top of the window, click on the 'Computer' tab, then select 'Map network drive'.
4. Choose a drive letter from the dropdown menu.
5. In the 'Folder' field, enter the path to the shared drive (e.g., \\ServerName\SharedFolder) and check 'Reconnect at sign-in' if you want it to be persistent.
6. Click 'Finish'.
7. If prompted, enter your network credentials (username and password).
8. The shared drive should now appear under 'This PC' in File Explorer.

macOS:
1. Open 'Finder' from the Dock.
2. In the menu bar at the top of the screen, click on 'Go', then select 'Connect to Server...'.
3. In the 'Server Address' field, enter the path to the shared drive (e.g., smb://ServerName/SharedFolder).
4. Click 'Connect'.
5. If prompted, enter your network credentials (username and password).
6. Choose the shared drive you want to access from the available options and click 'OK'.
7. The shared drive will now appear in the Finder sidebar under 'Shared'.
8. You can access the files as needed.
